summary: Install-time clone of mutable personal-fork branch ships unverified code into the wheel
details: setup.py executes `git clone --depth 1 --branch dev-3.12 https://github.com/gabry-lab/index-tts` during sdist install / wheel build, then copies the cloned tree into the wheel as `src/indextts` so it is imported by consumers of this package. The branch reference (`dev-3.12`, overridable via the `INDEXTTS_REF` env var) is mutable — no commit SHA, no hash, no signature verification — so the fork owner can change HEAD at any time and have arbitrary Python code shipped to anyone installing this pinned version 0.1.5. Compounding the risk, README and PKG-INFO advertise the upstream project `https://github.com/index-tts/index-tts`, but the actual fetch target is a personal fork at `https://github.com/gabry-lab/index-tts` that the package's documentation never mentions. Installers auditing the README cannot see where the executed code actually comes from. The fetch fires automatically during sdist install (PEP 517 build backend path), satisfying the auto-execute-on-default-install gate for this class.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
